items.title: Beyond Independent Frames: Latent Attention Masked Autoencoders for Multi-View Echocardiography
items.abstract: This paper introduces the Latent Attention Masked Autoencoder (LAMAE), a foundation model designed to overcome the limitations of standard Masked Autoencoders in processing multi-view echocardiography. LAMAE incorporates a latent attention module to facilitate information exchange across different frames and views directly in the latent space. This enables the model to learn holistic cardiac representations by effectively aggregating variable-length, heterogeneous observational data.
items.figure_caption: LAMAE architecture overview . During pretraining ( left ), masked frames from multiple views are encoded and fused through the Latent Attention (LA) module to learn shared representations, which are used to reconstruct full frames. During finetuning ( right ), frames are processed through that same encoder and LA module, followed by a lightweight classification head.

items.title: Robustness of Vision Foundation Models to Common Perturbations
items.abstract: This paper systematically studies the robustness of vision foundation models to common image perturbations like JPEG compression and brightness changes, finding that these models are generally non-robust. The authors introduce three new robustness metrics, analyze their mathematical properties, and demonstrate that low robustness correlates with degraded downstream task performance. They conclude by proposing a fine-tuning method to enhance robustness without sacrificing utility.
items.figure_caption: An example for the worst-robustness property.

items.title: An Analysis of Regularization and Fokker-Planck Residuals in Diffusion Models for Image Generation
items.abstract: This paper analyzes the trade-off between regularization techniques and the Fokker-Planck (FP) equation residuals in diffusion models for image generation. The authors empirically investigate lightweight regularizers as alternatives to directly penalizing FP deviations, which is computationally expensive. They demonstrate that simpler regularization methods can achieve similar benefits in reducing FP residuals and improving sample quality at a significantly lower computational cost.
items.figure_caption: Noise injection in the forward process following ( 1 ) (top) and image generation from noise according to ( 4 ) (bottom).

items.title: OmniLight: One Model to Rule All Lighting Conditions
items.abstract: OmniLight is a generalized model designed to handle diverse adverse lighting conditions, including shadows and irregular illumination, by unifying multiple restoration tasks. Its core method involves extending the specialized DINOLight framework with a novel **Wavelet Domain Mixture-of-Experts (WD-MoE)** layer. This contribution enables OmniLight to be trained across all datasets simultaneously, achieving robust performance across various lighting domains rather than specializing in just one.

items.title: UniDoc-RL: Coarse-to-Fine Visual RAG with Hierarchical Actions and Dense Rewards
items.abstract: UniDoc-RL proposes a unified reinforcement learning framework for visual RAG, enabling a Large Vision-Language Model agent to sequentially acquire visual evidence. The core method uses a hierarchical action space to move from coarse document retrieval to fine-grained image selection and region cropping, effectively suppressing irrelevant information. This approach is trained end-to-end using a dense multi-reward scheme, contributing a more precise and context-aware visual information gathering process for complex reasoning.
items.figure_caption: Three critical factors for Visual RAG. UniDoc-RL address these challenges through the (a) Precise Selection action to bridge the semantic gap between coarse retrieval and reasoning, and an (b) Active Visual Perception action to focus on information-dense regions, both optimized via a (c) Dense multi-reward mechanism.

items.title: Why Do Vision Language Models Struggle To Recognize Human Emotions?
items.abstract: This paper investigates why Vision-Language Models (VLMs) struggle with dynamic facial emotion recognition (DFER). The authors find two key vulnerabilities: VLM pre-training exacerbates the long-tail distribution of emotion datasets, causing rare emotions to be misclassified as common ones. Their contribution is proposing alternative sampling strategies to mitigate this head-class bias.
items.figure_caption: Qualitative Failure Analysis of SOTA VLMs on Video Emotion Recognition. VLMs like Gemini2.5-Flash, Qwen2.5-VL and EmotionQwen misclassify emotions by failing to capture subtle, temporal cues. Examples include overlooking a brief smile (top row, ‘Happiness’ misclassified as ‘Sadness/Neutral/Disgust’) or a tensed facial expression (bottom row, ‘Anger’ misclassified as ‘Neutral/Anxiety’)

items.title: RadAgent: A tool-using AI agent for stepwise interpretation of chest computed tomography
items.abstract: RadAgent is a tool-using AI agent designed to generate Chest CT reports through a stepwise, interpretable process. Its core contribution is providing a fully inspectable trace of intermediate decisions and tool interactions, allowing clinicians to validate the reasoning behind the final output. This approach significantly improves clinical accuracy and robustness compared to existing 3D Vision-Language Model counterparts.
items.figure_caption: Overview of RadAgent . a Given a 3D CT volume and a user query, RadAgent first produces an initial report draft and then enters an agent loop guided by a clinician-inspired diagnostic checklist. At each step, the agent plans the next diagnostic action, selects an appropriate tool from a toolbox, updates its memory with tool outputs, and refines its findings until sufficient evidence is collected for generating the final report. b An illustrative example trace showing how the agent verifies preliminary findings through sequential tool calls and accumulates evidence into a complete report. c Training and evaluation pipeline, including composite reward design, clinician-reviewed checklist construction, GRPO-based agent training, and evaluation on internal and external chest CT benchmarks.

items.description: AI You Control: Choose your models. Own your data. Eliminate vendor lock-in.
items.summary: This repository, "sponsors/thunderbird," focuses on providing an AI platform where users maintain control over their models and data, ensuring data ownership and eliminating vendor lock-in.

items.description: AI that sees your screen, listens to your conversations and tells you what to do
items.summary: This repository, "sponsors/BasedHardware," describes an AI system designed to actively monitor a user's screen and conversations to provide real-time guidance or instructions.

items.description: A lightweight, powerful framework for multi-agent workflows
items.summary: The `openai-agents-python` repository provides a lightweight, powerful framework for building and managing complex multi-agent workflows using Python.

items.description: The GEP-Powered Self-Evolution Engine for AI Agents. Genome Evolution Protocol. | evomap.ai
items.summary: EvoMap/evolver is a self-evolution engine for AI agents, utilizing the Genome Evolution Protocol (GEP) to power its adaptive and evolving capabilities.

items.description: DeepGEMM: clean and efficient FP8 GEMM kernels with fine-grained scaling
items.summary: DeepGEMM provides clean and efficient FP8 General Matrix Multiplication (GEMM) kernels featuring fine-grained scaling for optimized performance.

items.description: 《动手学大模型Dive into LLMs》系列编程实践教程
items.summary: This repository provides hands-on programming tutorials accompanying the "Dive into LLMs" series, focusing on practical implementation of large language models.

items.description: Claude Desktop for Debian-based Linux distributions
items.summary: This repository provides a desktop application for interacting with Claude AI, specifically packaged for Debian-based Linux distributions.

items.description: An open-source remote desktop application designed for self-hosting, as an alternative to TeamViewer.
items.summary: RustDesk is an open-source, self-hostable remote desktop application providing a secure alternative to proprietary solutions like TeamViewer.

items.description: Claude Code skill to support Android app's reverse engineering
items.summary: This GitHub repository provides a Claude Code skill designed to assist users with the process of reverse engineering Android applications.

items.description: Enterprise Architecture Governance & Vendor Procurement Toolkit
items.summary: Arc-Kit is an enterprise architecture governance and vendor procurement toolkit designed to streamline and manage architectural decision-making and the acquisition of necessary technologies.

items.title: Scan your website to see how ready it is for AI agents
items.summary: The website **isitagentready.com** offers a tool to scan your website and assess its readiness for interaction with AI agents.
